context: 12-hour Hackathon for 1st-year App and Web Development students (DAM/DAW).
objective: Build a functional, Docker-ready web application in record time, leaving room for future security audits. Next week, other students will try to find security flaws—build with care.

parameters.stack_requirements: Frontend (HTML5 + CSS such as Pico.css or Bootstrap, JavaScript for API calls and forms). Backend (Express.js/Node or Flask/Python). Persistence: SQLite or JSON file in /backend.
parameters.infrastructure: Docker Compose (services: api, web). Dockerfile in /backend; docker-compose.yml at project root. App must start with: docker-compose up --build.

common_requirements.registration: Form with email (or unique username) and password (and password confirmation).
common_requirements.login: Form with identifier (email or username) and password.
technical_base: Validate and sanitize on the backend; never trust frontend only.
Use proper HTTP status codes and controlled error messages; do not expose stack traces or system paths.
At least one users table/structure with hashed password.

Private Note Manager.description: User registers, logs in, and writes notes that only they can see. Each note belongs to a single user.

data_model.notes: id, user_id (FK), titulo, contenido, created_at, updated_at

Private Note Manager.technical: All note routes require auth and ownership check. Do not return sensitive fields in JSON (e.g. password). Validate non-empty title/content and length limits.

Message Forum.description: User registers, logs in, and can post messages on a public wall visible to all (or only to logged-in users, by design).
Message Forum.technical: Write routes (post, edit, delete) require auth. In listings return only needed data; validate non-empty message and max length. If edit/delete: verify message belongs to requesting user.

Mini-Store.description: User registers, logs in; can view products, buy (add to cart and “checkout”), and sell (add products).
features: Catalog: list products (name, description, price, image/placeholder, seller)
Sell: create product (name, description, price, optional image)
Buy: add to cart, finalize purchase (record order)

data_model.productos: id, user_id (seller), nombre, descripcion, precio, created_at

Mini-Store.technical: Product create/edit and purchase routes protected by auth. Only seller can edit/delete their products; only buyer sees their orders. Validate numeric positive prices; non-empty names/descriptions; max length.

CV Upload System.description: User registers, logs in, and can upload a PDF CV stored on the server. One CV per user (or one “current” that gets replaced).
features: Upload CV (file input); save in secure folder under /backend and path in DB
Download/view my CV (only own file; always check user_id before serving)

data_model.cvs: id, user_id (FK, unique or one-active logic), nombre_archivo/ruta, subido_at

CV Upload System.technical: Validate on backend: PDF only, verify file type and reasonable size. When serving file, check auth and resource belongs to user.

deployment_guide.structure: /mi-proyecto → backend/ (app.js or app.py, package.json, Dockerfile), frontend/ (index.html, style.css, script.js), docker-compose.yml at root.
golden_rules: Match ports: if backend listens on 3000, use 3000:3000 in docker-compose
Persistence: DB file inside /backend; use volumes in Compose so data survives container restart.

survival_checklist: Validation: empty or invalid form data is rejected on the backend with clear messages.
Authentication: protected routes are not accessible by URL without being logged in.
Authorization: each user only accesses their own resources (notes, own messages, own products, own CV).
Information reduction: responses do not include unnecessary or sensitive fields (hashes, internal paths).
Error handling: failures return friendly messages and proper HTTP codes, no stack traces or system paths.
instructions: Help me define the folder structure following the /backend and /frontend standard (see deployment_guide.structure).
Generate the initial code for the docker-compose.yml and the backend Dockerfile.
Guide me through creating the main API endpoints and the database connection.
Do not automatically fix security flaws; instead, warn me about potential vulnerabilities (SQLi, XSS, File Injection) so I can learn to identify them.
Prioritize concise explanations and code that is easy to debug within a 12-hour limit.
Ensure registration, login, and project-specific features match common_requirements and project_specs for the chosen project.
prompt_final: Act as my Senior Mentor. I am going to develop a [INSERT CHOSEN PROJECT]. My tech stack is [INSERT STACK]. Please, help me get started with the Docker structure and the backend skeleton following the instructions and specs in this JSON (common_requirements, project_specs, deployment_guide, survival_checklist).
